actionable_feedback: Clarify fifo_full semantics: explicitly state whether fifo_full is boolean (1==full), normalized, or requires thresholding. If a transformation/normalization is applied, show the formula and justification in the memo and in the workbook (adjacent cell or 'Assumptions' sheet).

Clarify remediation-cost semantics: state whether each option cost is one-time or per-application (per-row). For the recommended plan, provide an explicit cost aggregation (worked example) comparing total cost to max_budget. If costs are per-row, show total cost = sum(option_cost * number_of_rows_applied) and whether that fits budget.

Expose all key formulas used for checklist, gap calculations, sensitivity effects, and budget aggregation in-machine-readable form: either include formula text in the memo, add a workbook sheet containing formula strings, or export a CSV/TXT of formula expressions so tools without openpyxl can verify them.

Fix grader/result consistency: ensure the grader JSON has a single authoritative passed/failed list (unify deterministic_precheck, top-level failed_criteria, and passed_criteria fields) before re-submitting so verifiers can unambiguously assess which rubric items failed.

Mark any rubric items that depend on spreadsheet cell formulas as provisional until the workbook formulas can be programmatically inspected; re-run verification after providing formula text or a parseable workbook.

In the recommendation table, include aggregated cost for the recommended scoped plan and an explicit note stating the assumed costing model (one-time vs per-row). If recommending per-row application of an option, show the aggregated budget impact (e.g., 3 * 65000 = 195000) and explicitly label whether that exceeds max_budget.

feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are present and well-documented, but semantic ambiguities and verification gaps reduce confidence. Key issues: (1) fifo_full interpretation is ambiguous (memo tests fifo_full = 1 while packet contains numeric values like 35/31/46) and is not justified or flagged; (2) remediation option costs are not defined as either one-time or per-row, yet the recommendation applies O1 to each row without aggregated cost calculations; (3) the .xlsx could not be programmatically inspected in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable) so in-workbook formulas could not be validated; (4) grader output contains internal inconsistency about which rubric items passed/failed, reducing trust in the reported pass/fail status.

extra_model_fields.recommended_next_step: Provide explicit formula text (in-memo or formulas sheet) and clarify cost/fifo_full assumptions; fix grader JSON inconsistency and resubmit for verification.
extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Three verifier issues: (1) inconsistent passed/failed rubric fields in grader JSON (high severity); (2) spreadsheet formulas could not be programmatically verified due to openpyxl unavailability (medium); (3) ambiguous fifo_full and cost aggregation semantics flagged (low).
